title: Harmonia Village
description: First village. Elder Theron who's permanently exhausted by heroism. Scholar Milo who invented the autobattle journal. Phil the Lost who remembers things he shouldn't. Marta at the Sleepy Slime. A merchant who's sixty percent sure his swords work.

steps.text: Harmonia was the kind of village where nothing happened at dawn. Things happened at a responsible mid-morning hour, after porridge and before the first ale. The party arrived at neither of these times.

lines.speaker: Elder Theron
lines.text: The cave at the mountain's base has been spitting out monsters for three weeks. The real army is busy protecting the chancellor. The fake army — that's you — is what we have.

lines.speaker: Elder Theron
lines.text: There's a man in the village — Scholar Milo — who has theories about combat. Strange theories. I recommend listening. I also recommend not asking follow-up questions.

lines.speaker: Scholar Milo
lines.text: Before you go — have you considered the philosophical implications of combat automation?

lines.speaker: Scholar Milo
lines.text: Imagine a world where you don't fight your battles. You design them. You build a system of rules — if this, then that — and the system fights for you.

lines.speaker: Scholar Milo
lines.text: Chapter one is about letting go. Chapter two is about letting go faster. Chapter three is about accepting that the gap between letting go and having it taken from you is smaller than most people think.

lines.speaker: Phil the Lost
lines.text: Phil. Phil the Lost. Called that because I lose things. Mostly I lose track of what's happened before.

lines.speaker: Phil the Lost
lines.text: Before anything much. The village. The seasons. You know how it is — years blur together. You forget which winter was which.

lines.speaker: Phil the Lost
lines.text: Good luck with the cave. Try not to die in the first room. It's bad for the spirit.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: The Sleepy Slime has beds, mead, and porridge. The beds are adequate. The mead is good. The porridge is what it is.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: If you're the ones going to the cave, eat first. Nobody fights well on an empty stomach, and whatever's in that cave has been making my supply deliveries disappear.

lines.speaker: Merchant
lines.text: Welcome to Ye Olde Definitely Legitimate Armoury. Every sword comes with a guarantee that it is, in fact, a sword.

lines.speaker: Merchant
lines.text: Testing? No. We don't test. Testing implies doubt, and doubt is bad for business. These swords are swords. That's all you need to know.
